_instructions: This file contains all my projects. Edit this file to add,
remove, or update projects on my portfolio.
IMPORTANT: This is a JSON file. Be careful with commas!
- Each project needs a comma after it EXCEPT the last one
- Each field needs a comma after it EXCEPT the last one in that project
- id: A unique identifier (any text, no spaces)
- technologies: An array of tech used ['Tech1', 'Tech2']
- image: Path to project image (put images in assets/projects/)
- github: GitHub URL (typically for personal projects)
TO ADD A PROJECT: Copy one of the examples below and modify it
TO REMOVE A PROJECT: Delete the entire {...} block (and fix commas)

projects.title: Generative AI for Data Analytics
description: Problem: Data validation team spending days manually checking 10M+ records for anomalies across Snowflake pipelines — errors caught too late to prevent downstream business impact.
Architecture decision: Chose Claude API for structured JSON output reliability over GPT-4; used Snowflake Cortex (Mistral-large2) for in-warehouse inference to avoid data egress costs and latency.
What broke: Initial prompts hallucinated valid anomaly classifications on edge cases with null-heavy columns — fixed by adding schema-aware few-shot examples with explicit null handling instructions.
Metric: 70% improvement in anomaly detection accuracy across 10M+ records; analytics cycle compressed from days to 3 hours for 50+ stakeholders via automated ThoughtSpot dashboards.

projects.title: LLM Coaching App – Two-Stage Retrieval
description: Problem: Generic fitness apps return irrelevant exercises for specific goals — a user searching 'lower back rehab' gets generic gym results because vector similarity alone fails on short, jargon-heavy exercise descriptions.

Architecture decision: Two-stage retrieval (BM25 keyword search → Mistral-7B reranking via HuggingFace Inference API) instead of pure vector search; BM25 handles exact equipment and muscle-group terms better than embeddings on short text.

What broke: Mistral reranking collapsed to returning the same top 3 exercises for different queries when the prompt lacked diversity constraints — fixed by adding explicit 'no repeated muscle groups' instruction to the ranking prompt.
Metric: Built on a zero-cost stack (SQLite + HuggingFace Spaces); structured BM25 index with pre-built search_text column achieves sub-second retrieval with graceful JSON fallback handling.

projects.title: Dual-UMAP Multimodal Genomics Integration
description: Problem: Standard WNN fusion loses biological signal when integrating RNA and ATAC modalities — existing methods fail on 34,774-cell datasets because modality imbalance causes one modality to dominate the embedding.

Architecture decision: Novel UMAP extension with a Coupling Regularizer instead of standard weighted fusion — explicitly boosted cross-modal edges by 50% to enforce consistency without collapsing either modality's signal.

What broke: Naive sparse implementation still ran out of memory on Colab free tier at 10K cells — required full pipeline rebuild using muon/MuData lazy loading to avoid materializing the full dense matrix.
Metric: 58% Silhouette Score improvement over WNN baseline (−0.0123 vs −0.0293); RAM reduced 98.8% (96GB → 1.2GB), enabling analysis on a free Colab instance.

projects.title: Tiny Transformer – Shakespeare Text Generation
description: Problem: Understand Transformer mechanics from scratch rather than using HuggingFace black-boxes — most tutorials skip implementation details (attention masking, positional encoding, training stability) that matter for debugging real models.

Architecture decision: WordPiece tokenization (vocab=500) over character-level — better generalizes to unseen word forms in Shakespeare's archaic vocabulary while keeping the vocabulary small enough for a 722K-parameter model.

What broke: Attention entropy collapsed to near-zero in early training — heads were ignoring context and copying the input token. Fixed by adding gradient clipping and reducing learning rate warmup steps.
Metric: Validation perplexity 45.25 — 11x better than a random baseline. Trained with GPU acceleration and cosine annealing LR schedule.

projects.title: View Regression Automation
description: Problem: QA team spending days manually testing 1,000+ Snowflake views after every ETL migration — no systematic way to detect which views broke across dev, staging, and production environments.

Architecture decision: Auto-generated validation queries from table names alone rather than hand-coding test cases — made the tool usable by non-technical users without SQL knowledge, broadening adoption across the team.

What broke: First version flagged false positives on views with intentional null columns — added configurable null tolerance thresholds per project to distinguish expected nulls from data quality failures.
Metric: QA cycle time reduced 90%; manual testing effort cut from days to hours across 1,000+ production views. Selected as final university thesis (50% of semester grade).

projects.title: SkinWise – RAG Skincare Recommendation System
description: Built end-to-end LLM skincare consultant processing natural language queries over 500+ products using semantic vector retrieval — reduced product discovery from manual browsing to a single query with sub-second latency.
Stack: LangChain, ChromaDB, HuggingFace embeddings, PyTorch, Streamlit. Deployed live on HuggingFace Spaces.

projects.title: Drosophila Larval Brain Network Analysis
description: Built a connectome analysis demo exploring community detection and spatial organization in the Drosophila larval brain — developed independently as an entry point into computational neuroscience research.

Implemented Louvain algorithm and label propagation for community detection across annotated neurons, and applied K-nearest neighbors, Hopkins statistic, and Ripley's K function to quantify synaptic clustering patterns.
Optimized with vectorized NumPy operations and ball-tree nearest-neighbor search for efficient analysis on large graph-structured biological data.

projects.title: Book Recommendation Agent
description: Built LLM-based book recommendation agent that accepts natural language queries and provides personalized suggestions.
Motivated by the challenge of finding books that match specific reading moods and interests — leverages LLM reasoning to go beyond genre tags and surface thematically relevant recommendations.
Deployed live on HuggingFace Spaces.

projects.title: Colorectal Cancer Data Analysis
description: Analyzed colorectal cancer dataset to derive clinical insights through exploratory data analysis and statistical modeling.
Created interactive visualizations and plots for pattern detection and data exploration.
Built for Data Visualization course at Columbia University.

projects.title: Email Alerting System
description: Developed automated pipeline monitoring system in Python within Snowflake — queried execution logs and tracked daily status of all data pipelines across production environments.
Engineered proactive email alerts notifying stakeholders of failures within 120 seconds, including specific job names and timestamps.
Eliminated manual log checks and reduced downtime 40%+.

projects.title: GBS Reporting
description: Architected centralized Snowflake stage environment consolidating data from multiple subject area tables, streamlining access to vital business metrics for the GBS team.
Developed automated ThoughtSpot dashboards for daily source-to-target data comparisons, enabling real-time error detection.
Implemented self-service analytics, eliminating tedious daily manual table iteration and reducing reporting effort.
